title: A tour of MLIR: The Dialect Stack Everyone Depends On
summary: MLIR (Multi-Level Intermediate Representation) is a compiler infrastructure that uses a dialect stack to enable modular and reusable optimizations across hardware targets. This article explores how MLIR's layered dialects underpin modern AI/ML frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch, enabling efficient code generation for CPUs, GPUs, and accelerators.
